title: manifest-eyes / Claim
description: A claim is a write-once ledger entry asserting that a named manifest packet was executed and produced a named output. The ledger is append-only; corrections supersede prior entries by reference, never by overwrite.

claim_id.description: v4 UUID. The claim is identified by this UUID for the lifetime of the ledger.

claimant.description: Who or what executed the packet. Convention: '<model-name>:<role>' for model tiers (e.g. 'sonnet:generator', 'mistral-large:assessor-1') or 'human:<name>' for operator-executed packets.

kind.description: What kind of ledger entry this is. 'supersede' references a prior claim by claim_id and replaces it; the prior claim is retained for audit, marked superseded.

result_ref.description: Path / URI to the artefact produced. Required when kind='complete'.

result_hash.description: SHA-256 of the artefact bytes. Optional but recommended for any artefact that might be edited in place after the claim lands.

supersedes.description: claim_id of the prior claim this entry supersedes. Required when kind='supersede'.

consensus.description: Required when packet stage='evaluate' and kind='complete'. Captures the three-assessor outcome.

directive_001_floor.description: Self-grade against the four-floor numbers. TRUTH must be 100; the others are reported as integers 0-100 with floor markers. Output that does not meet floors cannot be 'complete'.

operator_override.description: If the claim was completed under operator override (skipping a stage), the verbatim justification is recorded here. The AI never writes this field.
